title: Using the FGCZ Quarto report templates
text: fgczquartotemplate ships one shared set Quarto report assets FGCZ analysis packages (ezRun, prolfqua, …) produce reports look feel. theme change becomes single package bump instead edit every downstream package. See layout live: https://prolfqua.github.io/fgczquartotemplate/example-report.html — full report rendered extension (tabsets, figure + callout rows, nesting, lightbox). pkgdown re-themes articles (forces theme: none template), standalone page — article — faithful demonstration FGCZ layout. article explains use templates.

title: Using the FGCZ Quarto report templates
text: Way 1 — Quarto extension (plain quarto render, R). per project: report header: Way 2 — R helper (stage files R, format: line needed) — subject rest article. routes produce report can coexist one repo. assets live inst/quarto/:
code: quarto add prolfqua/fgczquartotemplate --- title: "My report" format: fgczquartotemplate-html --- list.files(fgcz_quarto_dir()) #> [1] "_metadata.yml"           "fgcz_header_quarto.html" #> [3] "fgcz-plot-finder.html"   "fgcz.scss"               #> [5] "template.qmd"

what: Why the assets must sit next to the report
title: Using the FGCZ Quarto report templates
text: Quarto applies file named _metadata.yml automatically every .qmd directory (subdirectories) — styling attaches without front-matter reference. file turn names fgcz.scss fgcz_header_quarto.html bare filename: Quarto resolves names relative directory input .qmd, relative YAML mentions . three files must physically sit next report render time. exactly fgcz_copy_assets() fgcz_render() take care .
code: writeLines(head(readLines(fgcz_quarto_dir("_metadata.yml")), 30)) #> ## ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> ## FGCZ Shared Report Defaults #> ## Quarto applies this file automatically to every .qmd in this #> ## directory (and subdirectories) because it is named _metadata.yml. #> ## Individual reports need NO reference to it in their front matter. #> ## Keep fgcz.scss and fgcz_header_quarto.html next to it. #> ## ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #>  #> format: #>   html: #>     embed-resources: true #>     self-contained: true #>     smooth-scroll: true #>     page-layout: full #>  #>     # Code #>     code-fold: true #>     code-tools: true #>  #>     # Theme — flatly base + FGCZ overrides (tabset card styling for now) #>     theme: [spacelab, fgcz.scss] #>  #>     # Grid sizing #>     grid: #>       body-width: 1800px #>       sidebar-width: 250px #>       margin-width: 100px #>  #>     # FGCZ header #>     include-in-header: fgcz_header_quarto.html

title: Using the FGCZ Quarto report templates
text: _metadata.yml attaches directory, report carries FGCZ-specific front matter — just params title: Rendered bare quarto render CountQC.qmd, still picks full styling — package need even installed, long three assets directory.

title: Using the FGCZ Quarto report templates
text: fgcz_copy_assets() copies three styling files directory. stage temporary directory confirm landed:
code: dir <- file.path(tempdir(), "demo-report") dir.create(dir, showWarnings = FALSE)  staged <- fgcz_copy_assets(dir) basename(staged) #> [1] "_metadata.yml"           "fgcz.scss"               #> [3] "fgcz_header_quarto.html" "fgcz-plot-finder.html" file.exists(staged) #> [1] TRUE TRUE TRUE TRUE

title: Using the FGCZ Quarto report templates
text: fgcz_use_template() copies starter template.qmd directory together styling assets needs, runnable report one call:
code: qmd <- fgcz_use_template(file.path(tempdir(), "new-report"),                          to = "my_report.qmd",                          overwrite = TRUE) list.files(dirname(qmd)) #> [1] "_metadata.yml"           "fgcz_header_quarto.html" #> [3] "fgcz-plot-finder.html"   "fgcz.scss"               #> [5] "my_report.qmd"

title: Using the FGCZ Quarto report templates
text: fgcz_render() stages assets calls quarto::quarto_render(). needs Quarto CLI, call shown run vignette: single call downstream packages use place bare quarto::quarto_render().

what: Using it from a downstream package
title: Using the FGCZ Quarto report templates
text: Add fgczquartotemplate Imports (, since distributed GitHub, Remotes: prolfqua/fgczquartotemplate). Replace quarto::quarto_render() calls fgczquartotemplate::fgcz_render(). Leave report front matter free styling reference — staged _metadata.yml attaches automatically. Git-ignore staged copies (_metadata.yml, fgcz.scss, fgcz_header_quarto.html) render directory packaged assets remain single source truth.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Peter Leary. Author. Jonas Grossmann. Author. Falko Noé. Author. Witold Wolski. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Leary P, Grossmann J, Noé F, Wolski W (2026). fgczquartotemplate: FGCZ Quarto Report Templates Shared Styling. R package version 0.0.0.9000, https://github.com/prolfqua/fgczquartotemplate.
code: @Manual{,   title = {fgczquartotemplate: FGCZ Quarto Report Templates and Shared Styling},   author = {Peter Leary and Jonas Grossmann and Falko Noé and Witold Wolski},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9000},   url = {https://github.com/prolfqua/fgczquartotemplate}, }

what: FGCZ Quarto Report Templates and Shared Styling
title: FGCZ Quarto Report Templates and Shared Styling
text: One shared FGCZ look--feel Quarto reports (theme + header + defaults), reusable across ezRun, prolfqua, prolfquapp, …. Every report also gets right-edge 🔍 Find / 📥 Save toolbar — search figure table, bundle plots ZIP. 👉 See live example report — real layout, tabsets, figures, Find/Save toolbar. two ways use . Pick one.

what: Way 1 — Quarto extension (plain quarto render, no R)
title: FGCZ Quarto Report Templates and Shared Styling
text: Best render quarto CLI. Step 1. project, : Step 2. report’s YAML header: Step 3. Render: Done. ✅
code: quarto add prolfqua/fgczquartotemplate --- title: "My report" format: fgczquartotemplate-html --- quarto render my_report.qmd

what: Way 2 — R helper (stage files, then render)
title: FGCZ Quarto Report Templates and Shared Styling
text: Best render R (e.g. inside package). format: line, quarto add. Step 1. Install: Step 2. report’s YAML header — just , nothing FGCZ-specific: Step 3. Stage styling next report, render: Done. ✅ (fgcz_render copies _metadata.yml + fgcz.scss + fgcz_header_quarto.html next .qmd, Quarto applies automatically.)

title: FGCZ Quarto Report Templates and Shared Styling
text: produce report. can coexist one repo.

title: FGCZ Quarto Report Templates and Shared Styling
code: fgcz_render("report.qmd")               # stage assets + render (the usual one) fgcz_copy_assets("dir")                 # just stage the 3 files into dir fgcz_use_template("dir", "report.qmd")  # start a new report from the template fgcz_quarto_dir()                       # where the installed assets live

title: FGCZ Quarto Report Templates and Shared Styling
text: Quarto can’t reach installed R package fetch styling — files must sit next .qmd render time. Two clean ways get : Extension: quarto add drops _extensions/; opt format:. _metadata.yml: file exact name auto-applied every .qmd directory (format: line); R helper stages .

title: FGCZ Quarto Report Templates and Shared Styling
text: Edit fgcz.scss / fgcz_header_quarto.html inst/quarto/, run Rscript data-raw/sync_assets.R mirror _extensions/. Keep inst/quarto/_metadata.yml _extensions/fgczquartotemplate/_extension.yml step (format options, one flat / one nested contributes). Rscript data-raw/render_example.R regenerates live example report.

title: FGCZ Quarto Report Templates and Shared Styling
text: GPL (>= 3), matching ezRun.

what: The names of the shared styling assets — .fgcz_style_assets
title: The names of the shared styling assets — .fgcz_style_assets
text: files every FGCZ report needs alongside : directory metadata (_metadata.yml), SCSS theme, HTML header, search + download toolbar (referenced _metadata.yml bare filename). template.qmd deliberately excluded – starter copy , asset staged every render.

title: The names of the shared styling assets — .fgcz_style_assets
The names of the shared styling assets — .fgcz_style_assets
text: object class character length 4.

what: Copy the shared report assets next to a .qmd — fgcz_copy_assets
title: Copy the shared report assets next to a .qmd — fgcz_copy_assets
text: Copies shared styling files (_metadata.yml, fgcz.scss, fgcz_header_quarto.html, fgcz-plot-finder.html) installed package dir. _metadata.yml directory metadata, .qmd dir renders FGCZ styling (search + download toolbar) automatically. Call rendering, use fgcz_render(), calls .

title: Copy the shared report assets next to a .qmd — fgcz_copy_assets
Copy the shared report assets next to a .qmd — fgcz_copy_assets
text: dir Directory contains (contain) .qmd render. overwrite Overwrite existing copies dir. Defaults TRUE packaged assets stay single source truth.

title: Copy the shared report assets next to a .qmd — fgcz_copy_assets
text: Character vector copied file paths, invisibly.

what: Render an FGCZ Quarto report — fgcz_render
title: Render an FGCZ Quarto report — fgcz_render
text: Stages shared styling assets next input (via fgcz_copy_assets()) renders quarto::quarto_render(). .qmd needs styling front matter : staged _metadata.yml picked Quarto automatically, report stays fully portable.

what: Shared FGCZ Quarto report assets — fgczquartotemplate-assets
title: Shared FGCZ Quarto report assets — fgczquartotemplate-assets
text: package ships common set Quarto report assets inst/quarto/ FGCZ analysis packages (ezRun, prolfqua, ...) share one look feel:

title: Shared FGCZ Quarto report assets — fgczquartotemplate-assets
text: _metadata.yml Shared format defaults. Quarto applies file automatically every .qmd directory (subdirectories) reserved name – reports need reference . fgcz.scss Theme overrides (tabset/card styling, figure rows). fgcz_header_quarto.html FGCZ header injected via include--header. fgcz-plot-finder.html Right-edge search + download toolbar, injected via include--body. template.qmd generic starter report demonstrating tabset, figure--callout, nesting patterns. _metadata.yml applied directory, .qmd rendered plain quarto render picks FGCZ styling package involved front-matter reference – long styling files sit directory. _metadata.yml references fgcz.scss, fgcz_header_quarto.html fgcz-plot-finder.html bare filename, Quarto resolves relative input .qmd, must travel together; see fgcz_copy_assets() fgcz_render().
